author: Professor DR WISITH TUN-YHONG
executive_summary: The letter is largely incomprehensible and does not present any substantive, analyzable arguments regarding the SEC's NMS rulemaking. The author makes vague references to NMS rules, US Government fiscal benefits, Treasury and OMB, and a claimed 28-year trading research career. No clear policy positions, economic arguments, or actionable recommendations are articulated. The letter does not meaningfully engage with any specific provisions of the referenced rulemaking.
economic_arguments: Author vaguely claims that NMS-related benefits accrue to US citizens broadly via Treasury and OMB fiscal mechanisms, but provides no supporting analysis or data
Claims 28 years of trading activity constitutes a form of research, but no methodology or findings are presented
retail_investor_arguments: Author implies US citizens benefit directly and indirectly from the rulemaking but does not specify how or through what mechanism
recommended_actions: Author defers entirely to SEC decision-making authority
key_quotes: NMS acknowledgement that may over rules of act of whatever its doesn't matter the result of benefit its belong to whom may essential to focusing than another issues
the result of the study is allocating beneficial to US citizens long time for 28 years
If I break a rules then I apologized and I will respect all of you to make decision
respecting all decision outcome whatever be
